name: Camino Sanabrés and Agavanzal Reservoir Loop
name_original: Camarzana de Tera a El embalse de Nuestra Señora del Agavanzal — circular por el Camino de Santiago
description: This 22.2-mile (35.8 km) moderate mountain biking loop around the Agavanzal Reservoir offers scenic views and a balanced ride.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Camarzana De Tera offers diverse natural landscapes within the Benavente y los Valles region of Spain. The area is shaped by the Tera River Valley, providing scenic routes along its banks with riparian forests. Riders can explore varied terrain, from lush forests featuring evergreen oaks to expansive open fields. The region's undulating hills offer a mix of ascents and descents suitable for different mountain biking experiences. Best mountain bike trails around Camarzana De Tera The most popular mountain bike trail is Castro de las Labradas Loop from Brime de Urz, a 24.0 miles (38.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 28 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the local landscape around Brime de Urz. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is The Sierra de Carpurias to the Valle de Vidriales - circular, a moderate 21.4 miles (34.4 km) path. This route traverses the Sierra de Carpurias and the Valle de Vidriales, offering varied scenery. Local mountain bikers also love the Camino Sanabrés and Agavanzal Reservoir Loop, a 22.2 miles (35.8 km) trail leading through the historic Camino Sanabrés and past the Agavanzal Reservoir, often completed in about 2 hours 28 minutes. Mountain biking around Camarzana De Tera is defined by the Tera River Valley, diverse forests, and undulating hills. The network offers options for easy and moderate ability levels. The routes in Camarzana De Tera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a review. More than 10 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Camarzana De Tera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Camarzana de Tera?
A: The terrain around Camarzana de Tera is quite varied, shaped by the Tera River Valley. You'll find scenic routes along the river banks with lush riparian forests of alders and ash trees. The area also features undulating hills, offering a mix of ascents and descents, and distinct natural zones with evergreen oaks, broom, rockrose, and thyme. This diversity provides trails ranging from tranquil sections to more challenging paths through wooded areas and open fields.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the Camarzana de Tera area?
A: There are over 9 mountain bike trails around Camarzana de Tera listed on komoot, catering to various skill levels. These routes explore the diverse landscapes of the Benavente y los Valles region.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Camarzana de Tera?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail available. The region offers a mix of routes, with options for both easy and moderate ability levels, making it accessible for beginners to enjoy the natural beauty.

Q: What are some interesting sights or landmarks I can see along the mountain bike trails?
A: While exploring the trails, you can encounter several points of interest. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1233510846/camino-sanabres-and-agavanzal-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Sanabrés and Agavanzal Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3834108" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nuestra Señora del Agavanzal Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. You might also come across the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5509742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa Marta de Tera&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy a break at a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3858806" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picnic Area by the River&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1238579516/the-sierra-de-carpurias-to-the-valle-de-vidriales-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Sierra de Carpurias to the Valle de Vidriales - circular&lt;/a&gt; offers varied scenery on a loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1233718353/castro-de-las-labradas-loop-from-brime-de-urz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de las Labradas Loop from Brime de Urz&lt;/a&gt; is another popular circular option.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Camarzana de Tera?
A: The mountain bike routes in Camarzana de Tera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. More than 10 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes and well-maintained paths.

Q: Is Camarzana de Tera suitable for family mountain biking?
A: The area offers routes with easy and moderate difficulty levels, making it possible to find trails suitable for families. The diverse landscapes, including riverbanks and open fields, provide pleasant environments for family outings. Always check the specific route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's ability.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails?
A: While many natural areas in Spain are dog-friendly, it's always best to check local regulations for specific trails or protected areas. Generally, dogs are welcome on trails if kept under control, especially in areas with wildlife. Ensure your dog is fit enough for the ride and bring plenty of water for both of you.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Camarzana de Tera?
A: The region's climate, with its lush forests and open fields, suggests spring and autumn would be ideal for mountain biking. These seasons typically offer milder temperatures and pleasant conditions, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential colder, wetter weather in winter. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for mountain biking in the Zamora province?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for mountain biking on public trails in the Zamora province. However, if you plan to ride through private land or specific protected natural parks, it's advisable to check with local authorities or park management for any particular regulations or access restrictions.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Camarzana de Tera?
A: While specific parking areas for each trailhead are not detailed, Camarzana de Tera and nearby villages like Brime de Urz, where routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1233718353/castro-de-las-labradas-loop-from-brime-de-urz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de las Labradas Loop from Brime de Urz&lt;/a&gt; begin, typically have public parking available in or near the town centers. It's recommended to check maps or local information for designated parking spots close to your chosen starting point.

Q: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the mountain bike trails in Camarzana de Tera?
A: Camarzana de Tera and its surrounding villages offer local establishments where you can refuel. While specific cafes or restaurants directly on the trails are uncommon, you'll find options in the town centers for a meal or a refreshing drink after your ride. Consider places in Camarzana de Tera itself or nearby towns like Santa María de Valverde, which is a starting point for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1238655309/santa-maria-de-valverde-camino-sanabres-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María de Valverde - Camino Sanabrés Loop&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: This is a lovely place to have a picnic while doing the Camino de Santiago. It is right next to the Tera River and is shady.
The reservoir of Nuestra Señora del Agavanzal is located in the municipality of Olleros de Tera. It dams the waters of the Tera River.
A small town next to the Tera river valley, fertile lands that were occupied from the Celts. There is a beautiful Roman villa in the village with a mosaic floor.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to ride this loop is generally during the spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). These seasons offer pleasant temperatures and less chance of extreme heat or cold, making for a more comfortable experience. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring colder, wetter conditions.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The Camino Sanabrés and Agavanzal Reservoir Loop is categorized as moderate. While sections around the Nuestra Señora del Agavanzal Reservoir feature stable service roads, the overall route includes varied terrain that might be more challenging for absolute beginners. Riders with some mountain biking experience will find it enjoyable.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: You can typically find parking in the vicinity of Camarzana de Tera, which serves as a good starting point for this loop. Look for public parking areas within the village.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on the Camino Sanabrés and similar rural trails, but they should always be kept on a leash to ensure their safety and respect for local wildlife and other trail users. Please be mindful of any specific local signage.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to ride this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to mountain bike the Camino Sanabrés and Agavanzal Reservoir Loop. It's a public route, part of the wider Camino de Santiago network.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain biking loop?
faqs.answer: The loop offers varied terrain. Around the Nuestra Señora del Agavanzal Reservoir, you'll find stable service roads with pleasant views. Other sections, being part of the Camino Sanabrés, can include dirt roads, forest tracks, and potentially some scrubbier landscapes, transitioning from flatter plains to more undulating areas.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key points of interest include the scenic Nuestra Señora del Agavanzal Reservoir itself, offering beautiful views of the dammed Tera River. You might also pass a Picnic Area by the River, perfect for a break, and the charming settlement of Camarzana de Tera.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop incorporates significant sections of the historic Camino Sanabrés, also known as the Sanabria Way, which is part of the larger Camino de Santiago network. You will also be riding on parts of the Camino Sanabrés (East) and the Mtb Camino Sanabrés (Este) / Mtb Camiño Xabrés (Leste).

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation nearby?
faqs.answer: The loop starts and ends near Camarzana de Tera, a settlement where you can typically find basic amenities such as cafes, small shops, and potentially some local accommodation options. It's advisable to check availability in advance, especially during off-peak seasons.

faqs.question: What's an insider tip for riding this loop?
faqs.answer: For a smoother ride with consistent views, focus on the service road sections directly bordering the Nuestra Señora del Agavanzal Reservoir. These parts are known for their stable surfaces and picturesque scenery, making them particularly enjoyable for mountain biking.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking loop?
faqs.answer: On average, mountain bikers can expect to complete this 35.77 km (22.2 mi) loop in approximately 2 hours and 28 minutes. This duration can vary based on your fitness level, speed, and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.
